key_insight: DeepJEB++ grows a small set of jet engine bracket designs into a large, simulation-labeled 3D dataset by augmenting in 2D latent space first, then transferring the results to 3D.
engineering_relevance: It provides a practical path to build scalable, physics-labeled CAD/CAE datasets for solid mechanics and design optimization when 3D data and manual labeling are scarce.
method: Fine-tune a pretrained 2D latent diffusion model on multi-view renders and generate new views via latent interpolation.
Use a vision-language-model quality filter to keep manufacturable designs.
Lift validated images into 3D meshes with a domain-adapted generative foundation model, then auto-assign FE labels from detected interfaces.
evidence_signal: Starting from fewer than 400 seed designs, the pipeline produces 15,360 simulation-labeled 3D brackets, a 40x expansion, and the authors evaluate manufacturability, label fidelity versus SimJEB ground truth, and distributional consistency.
watch_item: Performance depends on reliable interface recognition and the quality of the 2D-to-3D lifting step, so label errors or geometry artifacts could propagate into the final dataset.
paper_title: DeepJEB++: Foundation Model-Driven Large-Scale 3D Engineering Dataset via 2D Latent Space Augmentation
